title: Telemetry sampling and cardinality control evidence pack

sampling_policy.event_capture: Recorder.Record appends every event to the in-memory log and forwards every event to configured sinks.
sampling_policy.trace_capture: Trace summaries are derived from the full in-memory event log rather than from a sampled trace stream.
sampling_policy.metrics_capture: Metrics snapshots are derived from aggregate recorder counters and current worker/control-plane state at scrape time.
sampling_policy.audit_fanout: JSONL audit sinks receive every recorded event; there is no configurable audit sampling or export throttling.

current_guardrails: Prometheus metrics export aggregate counters and gauges only; per-task and per-trace identifiers stay in event/debug payloads instead of metric labels.
Trace count is emitted as a single gauge derived from in-memory trace summaries.
Detailed task_id and trace_id values remain query-time payload fields on /events and /debug/traces endpoints.

reviewer_posture.current_runtime_posture: Treat the current telemetry path as local diagnostics and rollout evidence, not a production-grade observability pipeline.
intended_production_posture: Add an explicit sampling contract for traces, metrics, and audit export fan-out.
Introduce label-budget or dimension-allowlist policy before exporting tenant, task, or trace-scoped dimensions to shared backends.
Back telemetry with a remote-write or OpenTelemetry-native pipeline that defines batching, retry, and retention behavior.

current_ceiling: No configurable sampling policy exists for traces, event-derived metrics, or audit fan-out.
No exporter-side cardinality budget, dimension allowlist, or remote aggregation policy exists.
No OpenTelemetry-native collector, OTLP exporter, or backend retention contract exists for the current telemetry surfaces.
